1	package com.nutriops.app.domain.usecase.rules
2	
3	import com.nutriops.app.data.repository.RuleRepository
4	import com.nutriops.app.domain.model.Role
5	import com.nutriops.app.logging.AppLogger
6	import com.nutriops.app.security.RbacManager
7	import kotlinx.coroutines.Dispatchers
8	import kotlinx.coroutines.withContext
9	import org.json.JSONArray
10	import org.json.JSONObject
11	import java.time.LocalDateTime
12	import java.time.format.DateTimeFormatter
13	import java.time.temporal.ChronoUnit
14	import javax.inject.Inject
15	
16	/**
17	 * Rules engine evaluator supporting:
18	 * - Compound conditions: A AND (B OR C)
19	 * - Hysteresis: enter at threshold X%, exit at Y%
20	 * - Minimum duration: condition must hold for N minutes
21	 * - Effective time windows
22	 * - Rule versioning
23	 * - Back-calculation for any historical date range
24	 */
25	class EvaluateRuleUseCase @Inject constructor(
26	    private val ruleRepository: RuleRepository
27	) {
28	    data class EvaluationResult(
29	        val ruleId: String,
30	        val ruleName: String,
31	        val triggered: Boolean,
32	        val reason: String,
33	        val metricValue: Double?,
34	        val threshold: Double?,
35	        val version: Long,
36	        val evaluatedAt: String
37	    )
38	
39	    data class ConditionNode(
40	        val type: String,        // "metric", "and", "or"
41	        val metricType: String?, // for leaf nodes
42	        val operator: String?,   // ">", "<", ">=", "<=", "=="
43	        val threshold: Double?,  // threshold value
44	        val children: List<ConditionNode>? // for compound nodes
45	    )
46	
47	    /**
48	     * Evaluate all active rules against current metrics for a user.
49	     * Runs off the main thread on Dispatchers.Default.
50	     */
51	    suspend fun evaluateAllRules(
52	        userId: String,
53	        metricsMap: Map<String, Double>,
54	        actorId: String,
55	        actorRole: Role
56	    ): Result<List<EvaluationResult>> = withContext(Dispatchers.Default) {
57	        RbacManager.checkPermission(actorRole, RbacManager.Permission.EVALUATE_RULES)
58	            .getOrElse { return@withContext Result.failure(it) }
59	
60	        try {
61	            val rules = ruleRepository.getAllActiveRules()
62	            val now = LocalDateTime.now()
63	            val results = mutableListOf<EvaluationResult>()
64	
65	            for (rule in rules) {
66	                // Check effective window
67	                if (!isInEffectiveWindow(rule.effectiveWindowStart, rule.effectiveWindowEnd, now)) {
68	                    continue
69	                }
70	
71	                val condition = parseCondition(rule.conditionsJson)
72	                val triggered = evaluateCondition(condition, metricsMap)
73	
74	                // Apply hysteresis
75	                val lastMetric = ruleRepository.getLatestMetric(userId, rule.id)
76	                val wasTriggered = lastMetric != null && lastMetric.metricValue >= rule.hysteresisEnterPercent
77	
78	                val effectiveTriggered = if (wasTriggered) {
79	                    // Already triggered: must fall below exit threshold to un-trigger
80	                    val primaryMetric = getPrimaryMetricValue(condition, metricsMap)
81	                    primaryMetric == null || primaryMetric >= rule.hysteresisExitPercent
82	                } else {
83	                    triggered
84	                }
85	
86	                // Minimum-duration hold enforcement
87	                val durationSatisfied = if (effectiveTriggered && rule.minimumDurationMinutes > 0) {
88	                    val hold = ruleRepository.getConditionHold(rule.id, userId)
89	                    if (hold == null) {
90	                        // First time condition is met: record hold start, do NOT trigger yet
91	                        ruleRepository.upsertConditionHold(
92	                            rule.id, userId, now.format(DateTimeFormatter.ISO_LOCAL_DATE_TIME)
93	                        )
94	                        false
95	                    } else {
96	                        val holdStart = LocalDateTime.parse(hold.holdStartedAt)
97	                        val minutesHeld = ChronoUnit.MINUTES.between(holdStart, now)
98	                        minutesHeld >= rule.minimumDurationMinutes
99	                    }
100	                } else {
101	                    if (!effectiveTriggered) {
102	                        // Condition no longer met: clear any hold
103	                        ruleRepository.deleteConditionHold(rule.id, userId)
104	                    }
105	                    effectiveTriggered
106	                }
107	
108	                results.add(
109	                    EvaluationResult(
110	                        ruleId = rule.id,
111	                        ruleName = rule.name,
112	                        triggered = durationSatisfied,
113	                        reason = when {
114	                            durationSatisfied -> "Conditions met with hysteresis and minimum duration"
115	                            effectiveTriggered -> "Conditions met but minimum hold duration (${rule.minimumDurationMinutes}min) not yet reached"
116	                            else -> "Conditions not met"
117	                        },
118	                        metricValue = getPrimaryMetricValue(condition, metricsMap),
119	                        threshold = rule.hysteresisEnterPercent,
120	                        version = rule.currentVersion,
121	                        evaluatedAt = now.format(DateTimeFormatter.ISO_LOCAL_DATE_TIME)
122	                    )
123	                )
124	            }
125	
126	            Result.success(results)
127	        } catch (e: Exception) {
128	            AppLogger.error("RulesEngine", "Rule evaluation failed", e)
129	            Result.failure(e)
130	        }
131	    }
132	
133	    /**
134	     * Back-calculate: replay rule evaluation using historical metrics and
135	     * the rule version that was active at that time.
136	     */
137	    suspend fun backCalculate(
138	        ruleId: String,
139	        userId: String,
140	        startDate: String,
141	        endDate: String,
142	        actorId: String,
143	        actorRole: Role
144	    ): Result<List<EvaluationResult>> = withContext(Dispatchers.Default) {
145	        RbacManager.checkPermission(actorRole, RbacManager.Permission.BACK_CALCULATE)
146	            .getOrElse { return@withContext Result.failure(it) }
147	
148	        try {
149	            val metrics = ruleRepository.getMetricsByRuleAndDateRange(ruleId, startDate, endDate)
150	            val ruleVersions = ruleRepository.getRuleVersions(ruleId)
151	
152	            val results = mutableListOf<EvaluationResult>()
153	
154	            for (metric in metrics) {
155	                // Find the rule version active at the metric's snapshot date
156	                val applicableVersion = ruleVersions
157	                    .filter { it.createdAt <= metric.snapshotDate }
158	                    .maxByOrNull { it.version }
159	                    ?: continue
160	
161	                val condition = parseCondition(applicableVersion.conditionsJson)
162	                val metricsMap = mapOf(metric.metricType to metric.metricValue)
163	                val triggered = evaluateCondition(condition, metricsMap)
164	
165	                results.add(
166	                    EvaluationResult(
167	                        ruleId = ruleId,
168	                        ruleName = "Historical",
169	                        triggered = triggered,
170	                        reason = "Back-calculated with rule v${applicableVersion.version}",
171	                        metricValue = metric.metricValue,
172	                        threshold = applicableVersion.hysteresisEnterPercent,
173	                        version = applicableVersion.version,
174	                        evaluatedAt = metric.snapshotDate
175	                    )
176	                )
177	            }
178	
179	            Result.success(results)
180	        } catch (e: Exception) {
181	            AppLogger.error("RulesEngine", "Back-calculation failed", e)
182	            Result.failure(e)
183	        }
184	    }
185	
186	    fun parseCondition(json: String): ConditionNode {
187	        return try {
188	            val obj = JSONObject(json)
189	            parseConditionObject(obj)
190	        } catch (e: Exception) {
191	            ConditionNode("metric", "default", ">=", 0.0, null)
192	        }
193	    }
194	
195	    private fun parseConditionObject(obj: JSONObject): ConditionNode {
196	        val type = obj.optString("type", "metric")
197	        return when (type) {
198	            "and", "or" -> {
199	                val children = obj.getJSONArray("children")
200	                val childNodes = (0 until children.length()).map { parseConditionObject(children.getJSONObject(it)) }
201	                ConditionNode(type, null, null, null, childNodes)
202	            }
203	            else -> {
204	                ConditionNode(
205	                    type = "metric",
206	                    metricType = obj.optString("metricType", "default"),
207	                    operator = obj.optString("operator", ">="),
208	                    threshold = obj.optDouble("threshold", 0.0),
209	                    children = null
210	                )
211	            }
212	        }
213	    }
214	
215	    fun evaluateCondition(node: ConditionNode, metrics: Map<String, Double>): Boolean {
216	        return when (node.type) {
217	            "and" -> node.children?.all { evaluateCondition(it, metrics) } ?: false
218	            "or" -> node.children?.any { evaluateCondition(it, metrics) } ?: false
219	            else -> {
220	                val value = metrics[node.metricType] ?: return false
221	                val threshold = node.threshold ?: return false
222	                when (node.operator) {
223	                    ">" -> value > threshold
224	                    "<" -> value < threshold
225	                    ">=" -> value >= threshold
226	                    "<=" -> value <= threshold
227	                    "==" -> value == threshold
228	                    "!=" -> value != threshold
229	                    else -> false
230	                }
231	            }
232	        }
233	    }
234	
235	    private fun isInEffectiveWindow(start: String?, end: String?, now: LocalDateTime): Boolean {
236	        if (start == null || end == null) return true
237	        return try {
238	            val windowStart = LocalDateTime.parse(start)
239	            val windowEnd = LocalDateTime.parse(end)
240	            now in windowStart..windowEnd
241	        } catch (_: Exception) {
242	            true
243	        }
244	    }
245	
246	    private fun getPrimaryMetricValue(condition: ConditionNode, metrics: Map<String, Double>): Double? {
247	        return when (condition.type) {
248	            "metric" -> metrics[condition.metricType]
249	            "and", "or" -> condition.children?.firstOrNull()?.let { getPrimaryMetricValue(it, metrics) }
250	            else -> null
251	        }
252	    }
253	}

All 6 remaining issues are now fixed. Here's a summary:

---

### Fix 1: Image downsampling applied
`ImageLoaderModule.kt` — Added `Size(maxDim, maxDim)` and `Precision.INEXACT` to the Coil `ImageLoader.Builder` defaults using `AppConfig.IMAGE_MAX_DIMENSION_PX` (1920px). Every image decode request app-wide is now capped.

### Fix 2: Image picker wired in evidence UI
- **`AgentScreens.kt`** — Added `rememberLauncherForActivityResult(ActivityResultContracts.GetContent())` in `AgentTicketDetailScreen`. The "Attach Image" button launches the system picker; on result, the URI and file size are passed to `viewModel.addImageEvidence()`.
- **`UserTicketsScreen.kt`** — Same pattern: added launcher + `imagePickerTicketId` state + "Attach Image" button per ticket card.

### Fix 3: Swap authorization strengthened
`SwapMealUseCase.kt` — Added `verifySwapBelongsToMeal()` which confirms the `swapMappingId` is in the swap list for the `originalMealId`. Called in `executeSwap()` after ownership check, preventing IDOR via arbitrary swap IDs.

### Fix 4: Ticket/order transaction tests rewritten
`TicketOrderTransactionTest.kt` — Completely rewritten with:
- Correct `logWithTransaction` mock matching the actual `transactionBlock` parameter name
- Properly mocked `TicketsQueries` and `Tickets` objects
- Tests for: SLA first-response timestamp on IN_PROGRESS, resolution timestamp on RESOLVED, invalid transition failure, auto-approve vs pending compensation, encryption verification, audit log for status changes

### Fix 5: Rule hysteresis/duration tests rewritten
`RuleHysteresisDurationTest.kt` — Completely rewritten with repository-backed tests using mocked `Rules` objects through `evaluateAllRules()`:
- Hold start recorded on first met → not triggered
- Hold duration short → not triggered
- Hold duration met → triggered
- Condition lost → hold cleared
- Zero minimumDuration → immediate trigger
- Hysteresis stay-triggered / un-trigger via exit threshold
- Back-calc with two rule versions selecting correct threshold per date
- Effective window skip

### Fix 6: RuleEvaluationWorker hardcoded metrics removed
`RuleEvaluationWorker.kt` — Injected `UserRepository`. Periodic work (no `userId` in inputData) now iterates all active users. Per-user metrics are fetched from `RuleRepository.getMetricsByUserAndDateRange()` with latest-snapshot-per-type selection. Results are recorded back as snapshots. One-shot work with explicit `userId` still supported.

Now I can see the real problems:

1. **ImageLoaderModule**: The `.defaults(...)` approach doesn't propagate `size()` to image requests in Coil 2.x — `size` is per-request, not part of `ImageRequest.Defaults`. So `IMAGE_MAX_DIMENSION_PX` is effectively unused.
2. **Image picker**: The launchers and buttons ARE in the code already, but they need to be verified correct. The real gap is no centralized `AsyncImage` composable with size constraints for *displaying* evidence images.
3. **No centralized image composable**: The constant is referenced in the broken defaults builder but never used at actual image display sites.

The fix: replace the broken defaults approach with a proper Coil `Interceptor`, and create a centralized `DownsampledImage` composable that all screens use.

13	import com.nutriops.app.config.AppConfig
14	import dagger.Module
15	import dagger.Provides

17	import dagger.hilt.android.qualifiers.ApplicationContext
18	import dagger.hilt.components.SingletonComponent
19	import javax.inject.Singleton
20	
21	/**
22	 * Coil Interceptor that caps every image decode to [AppConfig.IMAGE_MAX_DIMENSION_PX].
23	 * If a request already specifies a smaller size, that size is preserved.
24	 * This guarantees all image loads are downsampled before entering the memory cache.
25	 */
26	private class DownsampleInterceptor : Interceptor {
27	    override suspend fun intercept(chain: Interceptor.Chain): ImageResult {
28	        val maxPx = AppConfig.IMAGE_MAX_DIMENSION_PX
29	        val request = chain.request
30	        val size = chain.size
31	
32	        val cappedSize = when {
33	            size is Size && size.width is Dimension.Pixels && size.height is Dimension.Pixels -> {
34	                val w = (size.width as Dimension.Pixels).px
35	                val h = (size.height as Dimension.Pixels).px
36	                if (w > maxPx || h > maxPx) Size(maxPx, maxPx) else size
37	            }
38	            // If size is Original or undefined, cap it
39	            else -> Size(maxPx, maxPx)
40	        }
41	
42	        val newRequest = request.newBuilder()
43	            .size(cappedSize)
44	            .precision(Precision.INEXACT)
45	            .build()
46	
47	        return chain.withSize(cappedSize).proceed(newRequest)
48	    }
49	}
50	
51	@Module
52	@InstallIn(SingletonComponent::class)
53	object ImageLoaderModule {
54	
55	    @Provides
56	    @Singleton
57	    fun provideImageLoader(@ApplicationContext context: Context): ImageLoader {
58	        return ImageLoader.Builder(context)
59	            .memoryCache {
60	                MemoryCache.Builder(context)
61	                    .maxSizeBytes(AppConfig.IMAGE_LRU_CACHE_MB * 1024 * 1024)
62	                    .build()
63	            }
64	            .diskCache {
65	                DiskCache.Builder()
66	                    .directory(context.cacheDir.resolve("image_cache"))
67	                    .maxSizeBytes(50L * 1024 * 1024)
68	                    .build()
69	            }
70	            .memoryCachePolicy(CachePolicy.ENABLED)
71	            .diskCachePolicy(CachePolicy.ENABLED)
72	            .crossfade(true)
73	            .components {
74	                add(DownsampleInterceptor())
75	            }
76	            .build()
77	    }
78	}

108	/**
109	 * Centralized image composable that applies [AppConfig.IMAGE_MAX_DIMENSION_PX]
110	 * downsampling to every load. All image display in the app should go through this
111	 * rather than calling Coil [AsyncImage] directly.
112	 *
113	 * The [DownsampleInterceptor] in [com.nutriops.app.di.ImageLoaderModule] provides
114	 * a global safety net, but using this composable makes the sizing explicit at each
115	 * call site and allows per-screen overrides via [maxDimensionPx].
116	 */
117	@Composable
118	fun DownsampledImage(
119	    model: Any?,
120	    contentDescription: String?,
121	    modifier: Modifier = Modifier,
122	    contentScale: ContentScale = ContentScale.Fit,
123	    maxDimensionPx: Int = AppConfig.IMAGE_MAX_DIMENSION_PX
124	) {
125	    AsyncImage(
126	        model = ImageRequest.Builder(androidx.compose.ui.platform.LocalContext.current)
127	            .data(model)
128	            .size(Size(maxDimensionPx, maxDimensionPx))
129	            .precision(Precision.INEXACT)
130	            .crossfade(true)
131	            .build(),
132	        contentDescription = contentDescription,
133	        modifier = modifier,
134	        contentScale = contentScale
135	    )
136	}

`AsyncImage` is only used inside the `DownsampledImage` wrapper itself. All display sites use `DownsampledImage`. The only direct `AsyncImage` call is the implementation inside `CommonComponents.kt:125`.

Here's a summary of what was done:

---

### Fix 1: Image downsampling now actually applied

**`ImageLoaderModule.kt`** — Replaced the broken `.defaults()` approach (which doesn't propagate `size()` in Coil 2.x) with a proper `DownsampleInterceptor` registered via `.components { add(...) }`. The interceptor:
- Reads `AppConfig.IMAGE_MAX_DIMENSION_PX` (1920) on every request
- If the requested size exceeds max, caps it to `Size(maxPx, maxPx)` with `Precision.INEXACT`
- If size is `Original` or undefined, caps it
- If the request already specifies a smaller size, preserves it

This is a global safety net — every Coil load in the app is downsampled.

### Fix 2: Centralized `DownsampledImage` composable

**`CommonComponents.kt`** — Added `DownsampledImage` composable that wraps `AsyncImage` with an explicit `ImageRequest` using `size(IMAGE_MAX_DIMENSION_PX)` and `Precision.INEXACT`. All image display in the app goes through this function, not raw `AsyncImage`. Accepts an optional `maxDimensionPx` override for per-screen customization.

### Fix 3: IMAGE_MAX_DIMENSION_PX no longer unused

The constant is now referenced in:
- `DownsampleInterceptor.intercept()` — global ImageLoader interceptor
- `DownsampledImage()` — default parameter for the centralized composable

It's used at both the infrastructure level (interceptor) and the UI level (composable), providing defense-in-depth.

### Fix 4: Evidence images displayed with downsampling

**`AgentScreens.kt`** — Added `EvidenceUiItem` data class, `_evidenceItems` state, `loadEvidence()` method called from `loadTicketDetail()`, and an "Evidence" section in `AgentTicketDetailScreen` that renders IMAGE evidence via `DownsampledImage` and TEXT evidence as `Text`. The image picker launcher (`rememberLauncherForActivityResult` + `ActivityResultContracts.GetContent()`) and "Attach Image" button were already present and remain wired.

**`UserTicketsScreen.kt`** — Imports `DownsampledImage` for future evidence display. Image picker launcher and "Attach Image" button were already present and remain wired.
